title: Contract-deployer: burn down ~1,700 eslint warnings (type-safety + async handling)
description:
Contract-deployer: burn down ~1,700 eslint warnings (type-safety + async handling)

Follow-up to [HOK-2102](https://linear.app/hokusai/issue/HOK-2102/contract-deployer-clear-eslint-debt-fix-3-deferred-test-skipped-bugs) (#146), which got `services/contract-deployer` to `npm run lint` exit 0 by clearing all error-level violations and **downgrading the high-volume, behavior-sensitive rules to warnings**. CI now gates on lint errors. This issue tracks burning down the \~1,700 remaining **warnings** and promoting the rules back to `error` as each category reaches zero.

## Current warning inventory (from PR #146)

\~1,714 warnings, dominated by the `@typescript-eslint/recommended-requiring-type-checking` family:

| count | rule |
| -- | -- |
| 394 | no-unsafe-member-access |
| 273 | no-unsafe-assignment |
| 254 | strict-boolean-expressions |
| 138 | no-console |
| 125 | prefer-nullish-coalescing |
| 125 | no-explicit-any |
| 114 | no-unsafe-argument |
| 104 | no-unsafe-call |
| 44 | explicit-function-return-type |
| 41 | no-misused-promises |
| 30 | require-await |
| 28 | no-floating-promises |
| 25 | explicit-module-boundary-types |
| 18 | no-unsafe-return |
| 4 | restrict-template-expressions |

The `no-unsafe-*` family (\~903) is the root cause ‚Äî it cascades from untyped values (`JSON.parse`, ethers dynamic access, loosely-typed jest mocks). Most of it disappears by typing the sources rather than each call site.

## Why this is its own effort (not done in [HOK-2102](https://linear.app/hokusai/issue/HOK-2102/contract-deployer-clear-eslint-debt-fix-3-deferred-test-skipped-bugs))

Eliminating these properly is a typing-architecture project: type every `JSON.parse` result, every ethers contract return, and the jest mock helpers; convert `||`‚Üí`??` only where the left operand is genuinely nullable (behavior-sensitive ‚Äî `0`/`''`/`false` differ); and resolve async handling (await/void/`.catch`) per call site. Mass-autofix is unsafe (e.g. `prefer-nullish-coalescing` and `strict-boolean-expressions` can change runtime behavior). It warrants careful, reviewable, incremental work.

## Suggested phasing (promote each rule to `error` in .eslintrc.json as it hits 0)

1. **Async safety (real-bug value, \~99):** `no-floating-promises` (28), `no-misused-promises` (41), `require-await` (30). Add an async route-handler wrapper for Express; `void`/`.catch` fire-and-forget calls deliberately.
2. **Type the sources (kills most no-unsafe-\*):** give `JSON.parse` call sites typed results / validators; type the ethers contract wrappers and the `tests/mocks/*` helpers. Re-measure ‚Äî the \~903 `no-unsafe-*` should drop sharply.
3. `no-explicit-any` **(125):** replace with real types / `unknown` + narrowing; then promote to `error`.
4. `strict-boolean-expressions` **(254) +** `prefer-nullish-coalescing` **(125):** fix per-site with behavior in mind; then promote.
5. **Style (**`explicit-*-types`**,** `no-console`**,** `restrict-template-expressions`**):** lower priority; decide whether to keep as warn or enforce.

Each phase can be its own PR. CI already fails on any new error-level violation, so progress is locked in as rules graduate to `error`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] All `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-*` resolved and set to `error`.
- [ ] Async rules (`no-floating-promises`, `no-misused-promises`, `require-await`) resolved and set to `error`.
- [ ] `no-explicit-any`, `strict-boolean-expressions`, `prefer-nullish-coalescing` resolved and set to `error`.
- [ ] `npm run lint` reports 0 warnings (or a documented, intentionally-`warn` remainder).

## Risk

Low per-PR if phased; the behavior-sensitive conversions (nullish, strict-boolean, async) need careful review. Non-mint-path code.

---

Parent epic: [HOK-2053](https://linear.app/hokusai/issue/HOK-2053/epic-pre-mainnet-test-battery-5-gates). Follow-up to [HOK-2102](https://linear.app/hokusai/issue/HOK-2102/contract-deployer-clear-eslint-debt-fix-3-deferred-test-skipped-bugs).
